documentation.readme: PhanTap is an ‘invisible’ network tap aimed at red teams. With limited physical access to a target building, this tap can be installed inline between a network device and the corporate network. PhanTap is silent in the network and does not affect the victim’s traffic, even in networks having NAC (Network Access Control 802.1X - 2004). PhanTap will analyze traffic on the network and mask its traffic as the victim device. It can mount a tunnel back to a remote server, giving the user a foothold in the network for further analysis and pivoting. PhanTap is an OpenWrt package and should be compatible with any device. The physical device used for our testing is currently a small, inexpensive router, the GL.iNet GL-AR150. You can find a detailed blogpost describing PhanTap here
usage_sections: capture traffic entering the network (the source is non RFC1918 and the destination is RFC1918), destination IP and MAC is our victim, source MAC is our gateway,
SNAT bridge traffic to the victim MAC and IP address,

arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need wireless_ble_ext, use phantap because PhanTap is an ‘invisible’ network tap aimed at red teams. With limited physical access to a target building, this tap can be installed inline between a network .
When you need a concrete invocation, run `network.loopback.device='lo'` as documented upstream for phantap.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `network.@device[0].ports='eth0'` as documented upstream for phantap.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `network.lan.device='br-lan'` as documented upstream for phantap.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `network.wan.device='eth1'` as documented upstream for phantap.
When chaining wireless_ble_ext tools, pair phantap with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `phantap --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
